description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

This is circular.' The HTML detail focuses on globe formula substitution (23.45° axial tilt). These are rela

An internal coordinate convention is not a phys

issues.description: Section numbering mismatch between HTML and DOCX in Part 4.5: HTML 4.5.3 = Gravity at Rim, HTML 4.5.4 = Solar Diameter. DOCX 4.5.3 = Solar Angular Diameter, DOCX 4.5.4 = Tidal Pattern. Kill-shot table cross-reference row 11 references Section 4.5.3 — broken in one format. Curmudgeon review: SEC-4.5.3/4.5.4.
issues.fix_details: DOCX infrastructure issue. Requires changes to build-doc-v4.js which cannot be auto-patched. Human must manually update the DOCX build script.

issues.description: SEC-4.5.9 CRITICAL FACTUAL ERROR: Section claims "d_geo is undefined" as the first technical argument. This is FALSE for V13. The dome's V13 coordinates page explicitly defines d_geo: EW_arc = 4×a×E(e²)×(Δlon/360)×(r_avg/a), NS = |r₁−r₂|, d_geo = √(NS²+EW_arc²), where a=20,015 km, e=0.66. A dome defender can immediately show we didn't read their page. This is the lead argument in the section — failing it damages the entire section's credibility.
issues.fix_details: Rewrite: d_geo IS defined but (1) parameters a=20,015 km and e=0.66 are fitted to WGS84 data, (2) inputs (lat/lon) are WGS84-derived, (3) n(r) remains undefined. The real critique is circular derivation, not undefined formula. Prose section issue — cannot auto-patch; requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.5.9 MAJOR CATEGORY ERROR: Comparing scaffold Euclidean distance (410 km) between Christchurch and Greymouth to TranzAlpine rail distance (223 km) is comparing straight-line distance to a winding mountain route. Correct comparison is to great-circle distance (~160 km), which shows 156% overshoot — even worse for our argument.
issues.fix_details: Replace rail distance (223 km) with great-circle distance (~160 km) as the correct comparison. 410/160 = 256% of great-circle distance. This actually makes the argument stronger. Prose section — requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.5.9 MAJOR OVERSTATEMENT: Claiming a genuine model would have "one version" is easily refuted (Newton, Einstein, Bohr all iterated their models). Dome defender can use this to make us look unreasonable.
issues.fix_details: Replace "one version" argument with the correct critique: overfitting known data vs. predicting new data. Scientific models iterate, but they iterate because predictions fail — the dome's V13 coordinate system was not motivated by a failed prediction. Prose section — requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.8 MAJOR SELF-CONTRADICTION: Section claims sun appears "50% larger at noon" AND "30% distance variation = 30% size variation" — these are mathematically inconsistent. If the sun is 30% closer at noon than at horizon, angular size increases by 1/0.70 = 43%, not 50%. The actual dome geometry gives a 48% reduction at 90° hour angle (sun at horizon vs. overhead in flat-earth geometry). Our text contains a numerical inconsistency in a section critiquing numerical inconsistencies.
issues.fix_details: Queued as EXP-004 for analyst: full rewrite of Section 4.8 solar angular diameter kill-shot

issues.description: SEC-4.8 MAJOR MISSING DEFENSE: Section never addresses the dome's pre-built aetheric refraction defense (AI Context Rule 11). A kill-shot that ignores the target's known escape route is ineffective. Dome can claim aetheric refraction corrects the observed size variation.
issues.fix_details: Queued as EXP-004 for analyst: full rewrite of Section 4.8 solar angular diameter kill-shot

issues.description: DOCX MISSING SECTIONS 5.14-5.16: The Word/PDF document (build-doc-v4.js) completely omits Sections 5.14 (Repository Infrastructure: Steering AI), 5.15 (The Repository Is the Model), and 5.16 (The Monitoring Infrastructure). These three sections contain the review's most original contribution — the inject_ai_layer.py analysis, curve-fitting exposure, and blockchain timestamping critique. Any reader using the Word/PDF document misses the entire repository code analysis argument. This has been flagged by the curmudgeon in 4 consecutive section reviews (SEC-5.14, 5.15, 5.16, and repeated in 5.16 with priority escalation to critical). Requires adding Sections 5.14-5.16 to build-doc-v4.js.
issues.fix_details: DOCX infrastructure issue. Requires changes to build-doc-v4.js which cannot be auto-patched. Human must manually update the DOCX build script.

issues.description: DOME SOURCE FILES NOT ARCHIVED: Sections 5.14 and 5.16 make specific claims about Python scripts (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py, monitor.py, recalc_v51.py, app.py, test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py) including exact quotes, line references, and formula citations. None of these files are preserved in raw-text/. If the dome author modifies or removes these scripts, our claims become unsubstantiated assertions — especially ironic given Section 5.16 criticizes the dome for not archiving its predictions. The -29.1 nT calculation in Section 5.16 cites monitor.py which is unarchived, making the specific formula unverifiable. Curmudgeon flagged this as the highest-priority unresolved citation issue.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-018. Requires fetching and archiving dome Python source files from GitHub, then updating prose in Parts 5.14/5.16.

The HTML correctly uses computed counts. Violates project rule on hardcoding counts.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing aetheric refraction defense: Section does not mention aetheric refraction, even though Section 4.9 explicitly lists WIN-017 (parallax) as depending on it. Should cross-reference Section 4.9 and explain why position-dependent refraction cannot rescue the parallax argument (refraction depends on observer position, not star distance).
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing New Horizons parallax experiment (April 2020): NASA measured stellar parallax from 7-billion-km baseline (Proxima Cen: 32.4 arcsec, Wolf 359: 15.7 arcsec). Physically impossible if stars are on a dome 9,086 km high. Baseline (7B km) is ~800,000 times the firmament height; measurement made from beyond any hypothetical dome, eliminating aetheric refraction as escape hatch.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing differential-parallax argument: In any small patch of sky, Gaia measures stars with parallaxes differing by 100-1000x. Any dome motion (rigid wobble, flexible undulation, or refraction) would affect all objects in the same direction similarly. Per-star parallax variation perfectly correlated with independently determined distance is impossible under any dome mechanism.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing from DOCX/PDF: Section 4.3 (Gaia kill-shot) appears in HTML but build-doc-v4.js has no corresponding section. The Word/PDF download is missing one of the six kill-shots entirely. Needs a new section added to build-doc-v4.js after the GPS kill-shot.
issues.fix_details: DOCX infrastructure issue. Requires changes to build-doc-v4.js which cannot be auto-patched. Human must manually update the DOCX build script.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions

issues.description: HOL-HIERARCHY (curmudgeon alert 2026-04-07T15:30): Argument hierarchy is inverted for persuasive force. Self-contradiction arguments (Schumann WIN-002, Tidal WIN-045/046/049/050/051, Gravity at rim WIN-067) are in Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions tab, 8th of 10 tabs). External falsification arguments (weaker — vulnerable to frame-of-reference objections) are in Part 4 (tabs 2-3). Readers encounter the weaker external arguments first and may never reach the dome's own geometry refuting itself. Restructuring Part 4 to lead with self-contradictions would significantly improve persuasive impact without changing any content.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-007. Requires structural HTML rewrite of tab ordering and section sequencing in generate-html.js.

issues.description: SECTIONS.JSON NOT INJECTED INTO HTML: generate-html.js checks SECTIONS_AVAILABLE before calling loadSectionsCache(), but SECTIONS_AVAILABLE starts as false and is only set to true INSIDE loadSectionsCache(). Circular dependency means sections.json content is NEVER injected into the HTML. All sections.json patches (EXP-001 through EXP-012, all prose improvements) are silently ignored. Fix: change line 1777 from "if (SECTIONS_AVAILABLE) { const sections = loadSectionsCache();" to "const sections = loadSectionsCache(); if (sections) {"

WIN-051 detail_evidence should be restructured to lead with eccentricity as crux: N2 uniquely requires lunar orbital eccentricity (e≈0.0549); N2/M2 amplitude ratio ≈0.19 encodes this (N2/M2≈3e). Dome never specifies eccentricity. Yeet: full restructure needed.

WIN-052 detail_evidence needs deeper RAR rebuttal: acknowledge RAR is genuine ΛCDM challenge (kernel of truth), explain dome provides no mathematical derivation (no aetheric acceleration formula, no RAR slope/scatter prediction), and strengthen galaxy ontology argument.

WIN-053 detail_evidence restructure: lead with explicit flux conservation derivation showing B_south ≈ 39 nT predicted vs dome's fitted 64,852 nT (1,660:1 self-contradiction). Current text has components but needs centralized derivation.

WIN-053 V50.6 argument reframe: instead of penalizing model evolution, focus on (1) the architectural change was retroactive/unmotivated; (2) new toroidal topology still fails via flux conservation. Curmudgeon notes current framing looks unfair.

WIN-054 El Gordo galaxy cluster needs major research: (1) verify if ΛCDM simulations accommodate El Gordo — if so, change verdict to Std Model Explains; (2) verify Kim et al. 2022 citation relevance; (3) establish measured velocity dispersion. Current evidence may strawman ΛCDM.

WIN-057 detail_extra is null. Should add free-parameter context: r_eq=14,105 km is 4th fitted parameter; OPEN-015 excludes near-equatorial cities (<12° lat), affecting ~40% of world population (Singapore, Bogotá, Nairobi). The free-parameter/OPEN-015 exclusion combo strengthens the curve-fitting argument.

WIN-058 unified angular coordinates: (1) quote exact V51.0 claim; (2) verify 0.9941 scale factor origin; (3) consider verdict change to Not Demonstrated. Current claim interpretation may not match dome's actual text. Possible WIN-057/058 conflation (ISS-383 pending-human).
